title: The Anxiety and Phobia Workbook
subtitle: Proven Methods to Overcome Worry, Phobias, Panic Attacks, and Obsessions
author: Edmund J. Bourne
themes: Anxiety as a manageable condition, not a personal failing
Workbook-based CBT: structured exercises for lasting change
The anxiety cycle: physiology, cognition, and behavior reinforce each other
Relaxation skills as the physiological foundation for managing anxiety
Cognitive restructuring: identifying and challenging distorted thinking
Exposure therapy: graded approach to feared situations
Acceptance and mindfulness as complements to CBT
Medication as one tool in a broader treatment plan
Lifestyle factors: exercise, nutrition, sleep, and substance use
Self-awareness as the first step to recovery
awards: Benjamin Franklin Book Award for Excellence in Psychology
Translated into more than a dozen languages
excerpt: Edmund J. Bourne's landmark CBT workbook has been the go-to resource for people struggling with anxiety and phobias for nearly three decades. This fully revised sixth edition offers step-by-step treatment strategies for panic disorder, agoraphobia, GAD, OCD, PTSD, social anxiety, and specific phobias — all grounded in the latest clinical research and structured around practical, fill-in exercises that readers complete at their own pace.
